definition: Percentage of a country's high-skilled (tertiary-educated, professional, or top-academic) population currently residing abroad. Used by Batista et al. to characterize cross-country variation in skilled outflows; rates can exceed 50% for top academics and >90% for PhD holders in small/poor countries.
coding_rule: Stock of skilled emigrants in OECD destinations divided by total skilled native-born population (resident + emigrant). Skill defined alternately as tertiary education, occupation (doctors, nurses, scientists, inventors, engineers), or top performance on entrance exams.
formal_definition: Emigration_rate_skilled_c = E_c^skilled / (E_c^skilled + R_c^skilled), where E is emigrants in OECD and R is resident skilled population.
operational_definition: Computed from OECD DIOC database, country census tertiary stocks, and occupation-specific registries (medical councils, IIT entrance lists, etc.).
measures: OECD DIOC tertiary emigrant stock / native tertiary population
Occupation-specific emigrant share (e.g., doctors trained in country X working abroad)
Top-N entrance exam alumni located abroad (e.g., IIT 100)

definition: Aggregate stock of skilled labor (e.g., licensed nurses, engineers, tertiary graduates) resident in the origin country at a given time. The central outcome of interest in the brain drain/gain debate.
coding_rule: Count of resident workers holding the relevant credential or skill in the origin country, measured before and after a migration policy shock.
operational_definition: Filipino licensed nurses (Philippine Commission on Higher Education), Indian computer-science workforce (NSS), tertiary-educated labor force (national LFS).
measures: Number of newly licensed nurses per year (Philippines example: 86,940 net gain over 2000-2006)
Tertiary-educated labor force size (national LFS)
Patent inventor counts by country of residence

display_name: Skill Acquisition Response to Migration Opportunity
definition: Behavioral increase in education or training enrollment in the origin country in response to a new migration opportunity to a destination that demands that skill. The mechanism behind 'narrow' brain gain.
coding_rule: Change in enrollment counts in skill-relevant programs following an exogenous expansion in destination-country visa availability for that skill.
operational_definition: Postsecondary nursing program enrollment in the Philippines tracked against US nursing-visa caps; computer-science enrollment in India tracked against H-1B caps.
measures: Nursing-program enrollment counts (Philippine Commission on Higher Education)
Computer-science enrollment / IT workforce growth in India

definition: Cross-border private transfers from migrants to households or institutions in the origin country. A major channel through which migration affects origin-country welfare.
coding_rule: Total remittances received by an origin country or province, measured in real local currency or USD.
operational_definition: World Bank bilateral remittance data; province-level remittance receipts from national household surveys; exchange-rate-driven shocks to remittance values (Yang 2008).
measures: World Bank Migration & Remittances data
Household survey remittance receipts (e.g., Philippine FIES)
Exchange-rate-driven income shocks to migrants

definition: Migrants returning to their country of birth after working or studying abroad. Carries human capital, capital, and norms acquired in the destination back to the origin.
coding_rule: Count or share of households containing at least one return migrant, or province-year flow of returnees.
operational_definition: Census-coded returnee households, expired-H-1B returnees to India, Chinese provincial Thousand Talents return programs, US-trained African scientists returning home.
measures: Census return-migrant flag
Chinese province talent-return program rollout dates
Expired H-1B return cohorts

definition: Stock of co-nationals resident in a destination country (or globally), often weighted by skill. Mediates trade, FDI, and knowledge flows back to origin.
coding_rule: Stock of foreign-born residents from origin country c residing in destination county d at time t.
operational_definition: US Census decennial / ACS foreign-born by country of birth at county level; ethnic enclave shares from WWII Japanese internment, Vietnamese refugee resettlement.
measures: US Census foreign-born by country × county
Historical ethnic-enclave shares (WWII internment, refugee resettlement)

display_name: Foreign Direct Investment to Origin Country
definition: Stock or flow of foreign direct investment from destination countries to the migrant's country of origin.
coding_rule: BEA bilateral FDI flows from US counties to migrant-origin countries.
operational_definition: BEA outward FDI by US county or state to country pairs.
measures: BEA outward FDI bilateral data

definition: Total value of exports and imports between origin and destination countries, hypothesized to be amplified by migrant networks.
coding_rule: USD value of bilateral exports + imports between origin country c and destination country d.
operational_definition: UN COMTRADE bilateral trade flows.
measures: UN COMTRADE bilateral exports and imports

definition: Number of patent applications or grants attributed to inventors resident in the origin country.
coding_rule: Count of patent applications filed at USPTO/EPO/WIPO with at least one inventor located in origin country c at time t.
operational_definition: USPTO PatentsView, EPO PATSTAT, WIPO PCT applications.
measures: USPTO patent applications by inventor country
EPO PATSTAT inventor country
Firm-level patent counts (Choudhury et al. 2024 India)

definition: Number of distinct international co-authors or cross-border research collaborations per scholar.
coding_rule: Distinct co-authors of a scholar with affiliations outside the scholar's country, per year.
operational_definition: Scopus/Microsoft Academic Graph co-authorship networks; bibliometric analyses for Chinese scholars and EU scientists.
measures: Scopus co-authorship distinct foreign affiliates
Citation impact relative to control group (Fulbright study)

definition: Rate of new firm formation in the origin economy, hypothesized to be sensitive to skilled emigration.
coding_rule: Annual count of newly registered firms per region.
operational_definition: Italian business registry; province-level new-firm counts.
measures: Italian Chamber of Commerce business registry

definition: Output per worker in the origin economy, sensitive to skill outflows and to firm capital-labor substitution responses.
coding_rule: Real value added per worker by industry-country-year.
operational_definition: Eastern European national accounts post-EU enlargement; Italian firm-level productivity.
measures: Eurostat productivity series
ORBIS firm-level value added per worker

definition: Quality of democratic institutions, electoral participation, and demand for public accountability at origin, hypothesized to be influenced by emigrant exposure to destination-country institutions.
coding_rule: Composite of V-Dem democracy indicators, voter turnout, and survey-measured demand for public services.
operational_definition: V-Dem electoral democracy index (Spilimbergo 2009); Cape Verde survey on demand for public services; village-level voter turnout.
measures: V-Dem electoral democracy index
Voter turnout by community
Survey demand for public services (Batista & Vicente 2011 Cape Verde)

definition: Shift toward more egalitarian household decision-making, female autonomy, and support for gender equity, hypothesized as a downstream effect of migration to liberal destinations.
coding_rule: Survey indices of household gender attitudes, female household-headship, and decision-making authority.
operational_definition: Cape Verde RCT spillover survey on gender attitudes; Bangladesh visa-lottery follow-up on female household headship and decision authority.
measures: Survey index of support for gender equity in household decisions
Share of households with female head
Share of women with exclusive decision authority

definition: Total fertility rate (children per woman) at origin, hypothesized to respond to migrant-transmitted reproductive-health norms.
coding_rule: TFR or completed fertility per woman by origin community.
operational_definition: DHS or census fertility by Filipino province linked to destination policy via SSIV.
measures: DHS / census total fertility rate

definition: Deaths per 1,000 live births before age 1 in origin areas; key population-health outcome in brain-drain debates.
coding_rule: Deaths < 12 months / 1,000 live births at province or country level.
operational_definition: DHS infant mortality rates; African country panel (Bhargava et al.).
measures: DHS infant mortality
WDI infant mortality rate

definition: Share of households with effective access to health-care services, hypothesized to improve with remittance income.
coding_rule: Share of households reporting any health-service utilization or insurance coverage.
operational_definition: Mexican ENIGH/MxFLS surveys; household-level health-care expenditure shares.
measures: Mexican ENIGH share of households without health-care access
Health-care expenditure as share of remittance income

definition: Real income or consumption per capita in households or provinces at origin, sensitive to remittance and migration shocks.
coding_rule: Real per-capita household income or consumption, in 2010 local currency or PPP USD.
operational_definition: Philippine FIES per-capita income/expenditure, real 2010 pesos.
measures: Philippine FIES per-capita income
Philippine FIES per-capita expenditure

definition: Share of children below the working-age threshold engaged in economic labor in the origin community. Often rises when household income shocks (e.g., loss of a migration earnings opportunity) force adjustment.
coding_rule: Share of children aged 5-14 who report working in the past week, by Filipino province.
operational_definition: Philippine FIES/LFS child-labor module by province.
measures: Philippine LFS child-labor rate

definition: Mean live-birth weight in origin households or communities, used as a proxy for prenatal nutrition and population health.
coding_rule: Mean grams at live birth, by household and migration-exposure status.
operational_definition: Mexican vital statistics linked to migration exposure.
measures: Mexican vital statistics birth weight

definition: Exogenous policy event in the destination country that changes the supply of visas or migration eligibility for citizens of an origin country. Provides identification variation in causal studies.
coding_rule: Discrete change in destination-country visa caps, eligibility, or freedom-of-movement rules.
operational_definition: US 2000-2006 expansion of nurse visas; H-1B cap changes; 2004/2007 EU enlargement; 1990 US Immigration Act; Vietnamese refugee resettlement; lottery randomization (Tonga, Bangladesh, NZ Pacific Access).
measures: US H-1B visa cap (annual)
Filipino nurse visa volume (CFO)
EU enlargement timing by accession country
Visa-lottery assignment indicator

definition: Responsiveness of origin-country training infrastructure (universities, vocational programs) to changes in demand for a skill induced by destination-country migration opportunities. A key moderator determining whether brain gain occurs.
coding_rule: Percent change in seats / enrollment capacity in skill-relevant training programs per percent change in destination-country demand for the skill.
operational_definition: Number of new private nursing schools in the Philippines per nurse-visa expansion shock; growth of computer-science programs in India.
measures: New nursing-program count (private vs public)
Engineering / CS seat counts
